facts: The patient's name is Julie.
The patient is a doctor.
The patient is female.

The headaches also occur when the patient is stressed.

The patient's headaches have increased in severity.

The headaches are located behind the patient's eye.
The headaches are described as a pulsing sensation.

The patient's headaches worsen with exercise.
The patient starts to experience headaches when jogging.

The ringing in the ears has been occurring for a few months.

The blurred vision is not constant.

The patient has papilledema.
Papilledema is swelling behind the eye.
The patient's cranial nerves are intact.
The patient's cranial reflex is normal.
The patient's gait is normal.
The patient's symptoms are concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension.
Idiopathic intracranial hypertension is a condition with increased pressure in the brain without a known cause.
The patient's symptoms are consistent with idiopathic intracranial hypertension.
The patient may need an MRI to confirm the diagnosis.
The patient may need a spinal tap to evaluate the pressure in their brain.
The patient may need to see a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam.
The patient may not need surgery for idiopathic intracranial hypertension.
There are medications that can help decrease the pressure in the brain.
Exercise and weight loss are important parts of the treatment for idiopathic intracranial hypertension.
The doctor will work on getting preapproval from the patient's insurance company for the MRI.
The doctor's team will help schedule the MRI once it is preapproved.
The patient will be called to schedule the MRI once it is preapproved.
